<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-07 -->

# V12: ESM Dual Package with Rollup Build

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The library historically shipped a single monolithic bundle for Node.js (with all dependencies inlined) and a separate browser bundle, both built with Babel + Webpack. This caused several problems:

- Modern ESM-first frameworks (SvelteKit, Nuxt 3, Vite) had compatibility issues with the CJS-only Node bundle
- Bundle size was inflated because dependencies were inlined rather than resolved at install time
- Tree-shaking was ineffective since bundlers couldn't statically analyze the CJS format
- The build pipeline was complex and hard to maintain
- `contentful-sdk-core` had already migrated to ESM-only, creating interop friction

Alternatives considered:
- ESM-only (no CJS fallback) — rejected because too many consumers still use CommonJS
- Keep Webpack — rejected due to maintenance burden and poor ESM output quality

## Decision

V12 ships as a **dual ESM + CJS package** built with Rollup:

- **ESM:** `dist/esm/index.mjs` (preserves module structure for tree-shaking)
- **CJS:** `dist/cjs/index.cjs` (single bundle for legacy environments)
- **Browser:** `dist/browser/index.min.js` (self-contained UMD for `<script>` tag usage)
- Dependencies are externalized in ESM/CJS builds (resolved via `node_modules`)
- The `exports` field in `package.json` controls all entry points
- Target is ES2021 with Node.js v20+ minimum
- Babel transpilation removed entirely

This aligns with the CDA SDK (`contentful`) which already uses this architecture, and with `contentful-sdk-core` which is ESM-only.

Source: commit `84a34310c` (feat!: v12 major release [DX-455]), internal planning docs.

## Consequences

- **Enables:** proper tree-shaking, modern framework compatibility, simpler build pipeline, smaller install footprint
- **Prevents:** usage in Node < 20 and browsers without ES2021 support (IE, Chrome < 85)
- **Trade-off:** deep imports (`contentful-management/dist/...`) no longer work — all public API comes from the root entry
- **Follow-up:** v11.x maintenance branch (`11.x`) provides 6-month security/critical fix window for consumers who cannot upgrade immediately
